channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Crews restore power to nearly all customers after island-wide blackout hit Puerto Rico
item.description: Following an island-wide blackout that hit Puerto Rico earlier in the week after a transmission line failed, power was restored on Friday to more than 98% of customers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Partisan divide widening on US support for NATO: Pew
item.description: A partisan divide is widening among Americans on the country's support for NATO, with Democrats being more likely to have a positive opinion of the military alliance than Republicans, according to a Thursday survey. The Pew Research Center poll found 66 percent of respondents think the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Venice expands day-tripper tax in bid to combat overtourism
item.description: VENICE, Italy (AP) — Venice is charging day-trippers to the famed canal city an arrivals tax for the second year starting Friday, a measure aimed at combating the kind of overtourism that put the city's UNESCO World Cultural Heritage status at risk.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Salvadoran president mocks Van Hollen, Abrego Garcia meeting
item.description: Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele took to social media late Thursday to mock Sen. Chris Van Hollen’s (D-Md.) meeting with Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was mistakenly deported by the Trump administration last month due to an "administrative error.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Rubio: US will 'move on' from Ukraine peace talks if progress stalls
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said early Friday that the U.S. may "move on" from facilitating Russia-Ukraine peace talks if negotiations continue to prolong.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 25 in Gaza and Huckabee makes first appearance as US ambassador
item.description: Israeli airstrikes across Gaza have killed at least 25 people including children as the new U.S. ambassador to Israel made his first public appearance in Jerusalem.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says Ukraine-Russia talks 'coming to a head' and 'no one is playing' him in push to end war
item.description: President Donald Trump says negotiations between Ukraine and Russia are “coming to a head” and insists that neither side is “playing” him in his push to end the grinding war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Protest letters from former Israeli soldiers lay bare profound rifts over the ongoing war
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — When nearly 1,000 Israeli Air Force veterans signed an open letter last week calling for an end to the war in Gaza, the military responded immediately, saying it would dismiss any active reservist who signed the document.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Carney says lower internal trade barriers will help Canada more than Trump's tariffs will harm it
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Mark Carney said Thursday eliminating trade barriers within Canada would benefit Canadians far more than U.S. President Donald Trump can ever take away with his trade war as he made his case to retain power at the last debate ahead of the April 28 vote.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Haitians face record hunger as gang violence grips country in throes of economic crisis
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — More than half of Haiti’s population is expected to experience severe hunger through June, and another 8,400 people living in makeshift shelters are projected to starve, according to a new report released this week.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says US-Ukraine minerals deal could be signed this month
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he expects to sign a minerals agreement with Ukraine in the coming days, a few weeks after an expected deal signing was called off following a contentious Oval Office meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, The Hill

item.title: ICC opens inquiry into Hungary for failing to arrest Netanyahu
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — Judges at the International Criminal Court want Hungary to explain why it failed to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu when he visited Budapest earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Noem threatens Harvard with ban on foreign student visas
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem threatened Wednesday to ban Harvard University from taking in foreign students amid a growing battle over the Ivy League school not accepting the demands of the Trump administration to change its policies.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Former MLS player Aaron Boupendza dies after falling from building, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former MLS player Aaron Boupendza dies after falling from building in China, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Power blackout hits all of Puerto Rico ahead of Easter weekend
item.description: All 1.4 million customers were affected. By late Wednesday night, crews had restored power to 175,000 customers, or 12%.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia-linked hackers targeting European diplomats with invites to bogus wine tasting events
item.description: A Russia-linked hacking group is trying to get European diplomats to download malicious software through invitations to fake wine tasting events, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American pastor kidnapped in South Africa rescued after deadly police shootout
item.description: American pastor Josh Sullivan, who was kidnapped from his church in the Eastern Cape Province last week, has been rescued by police following a "high-intensity shootout.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Maryland man's family questions death, says he didn't drown in Bahamas
item.description: Dinari McAlmont of Bowie, Maryland, was found dead April 5 near the Atlantis resort on Paradise Island, according to Royal Bahamian Police.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas rejects Israeli ceasefire, hostage return deal over disarmament demands
item.description: Hamas has reportedly rejected Israel's latest ceasefire offer, which included the return of humanitarian aid in exchange for nearly a dozen hostages and Hamas' complete disarmament.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US pastor abducted at gunpoint while preaching in South Africa
item.description: Four men stole two cellphones from members of the congregation and kidnapped the pastor in his own truck.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Top Hamas terror leader killed in 'precise strike' by Israel: IDF
item.description: Top Hamas leader Muhammad al-‘Ajlah has been killed in an Israeli strike, the IDF has announced. Al-'Ajilah was responsible for arming terrorists with weapons used to carry out attacks against Israeli civilians and IDF troops, according to the IDF.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin sees Trump as 'an easy mark': John Bolton
item.description: During Monday's meeting with the press, Trump said of Ukraine: "When you start a war, you've gotta know that you can win the war.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, WGN 9

item.title: Trump hosts El Salvador's Nayib Bukele amid deportation controversy
item.description: Since March, El Salvador has accepted more than 200 Venezuelan immigrants accused by the U.S. of gang activity and violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Russian missiles hit Ukrainian city of Sumy during Palm Sunday celebrations, killing more than 30
item.description: SUMY, Ukraine (AP) — Russian missiles struck the heart of the Ukrainian city of Sumy as people gathered to celebrate Palm Sunday, killing at least 34 people, officials said, in the second large-scale attack to claim civilian lives in just over a week. The two ballistic missiles hit around 10:15 a.m.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Iran and US envoys hold negotiation over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: Iran's state-run broadcaster revealed that U.S. Mideast envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi “briefly spoke" together.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation
